region_of_origin: Singapore (Atlantic salmon sourced from Norway, Scotland, Tasmania, and Singapore aquaculture)
food_subgroup: Baked Atlantic salmon fillet; standard café and restaurant preparation; 180g fillet reference portion

ontology.foodex2_description: Atlantic salmon, cooked — closest FoodEx2 parent for baked Atlantic salmon fillet
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to baked Atlantic salmon fillet with lemon-herb or butter preparation; A00LC (Atlantic salmon, cooked) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for baked Atlantic salmon fillet as a food product in the clinical terminology system.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant or café serving is a 180g baked Atlantic salmon fillet, typically served with vegetables or salad on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Salmon, Atlantic, farmed, cooked, dry heat — USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (FoodData Central — Foundation Foods), cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods entry for Atlantic salmon, farmed, cooked dry heat, adapted with HPB Singapore reference values. Lemon-herb preparation adds negligible macronutrients; values represent plain baked fillet. Butter preparation variant adds approximately 130 kcal and 8g saturated fat per 180g serving but base values reflect lemon-herb preparation.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates; GI testing not applicable
gi_source.source_match_notes: Baked Atlantic salmon fillet contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. Foods with no carbohydrate content are not assigned a GI value; glycemic_index is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel indicating not_applicable status, and gi_category is set to not_applicable. GL per 180g serving = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (59mg/100g), high potassium (490mg/100g), and omega-3 fatty acids (EPA+DHA ~2.2–3.5g per 180g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Plain baked Atlantic salmon is an excellent choice for hypertension management: one 180g serving provides only 106mg sodium while delivering 882mg potassium (25% of the 3,500mg daily target), and omega-3 EPA+DHA at therapeutic doses (≥2g/day) has demonstrated systolic blood pressure reductions of approximately 1.5–3 mmHg in multiple meta-analyses.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate (GL = 0), high complete protein (20.4g/100g), omega-3 fatty acids improving insulin sensitivity, and vitamin D (9.2mcg/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Baked Atlantic salmon is highly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes: it contains zero carbohydrate and zero glycaemic load, 38g complete protein per 180g serving maximises satiety and GLP-1 response, omega-3 EPA+DHA (3.4g per serving) is documented in RCTs to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce hepatic fat, and vitamin D (15mcg per 180g serving meets the full daily adequate intake) addresses the independent association between vitamin D deficiency and insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 EPA+DHA content (~2.2–3.5g per 180g serving), low saturated fat (3.1g/100g), high potassium, and selenium (36mcg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Baked Atlantic salmon is one of the most cardioprotective single foods available: one 180g serving provides 3.4g omega-3 EPA+DHA (exceeding the AHA recommendation of ≥1g/day for CVD patients), which reduces triglycerides, lowers resting heart rate, and reduces cardiovascular mortality risk; the low saturated fat content (5.6g per serving) and high potassium (882mg per serving) further support vascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 EPA+DHA (3.4g per 180g serving), vitamin D (15mcg per serving), and selenium (64.8mcg per serving)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: One 180g baked salmon fillet provides the full therapeutic omega-3 target (≥2.5g EPA+DHA/day) supported by meta-analyses demonstrating reduced swollen joint count, reduced tender joint count, reduced morning stiffness, and modest NSAID-sparing effect in RA patients; vitamin D (15mcg per serving) and selenium (118% of daily RDA per serving) provide additional anti-inflammatory support.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked Atlantic Salmon Fillet is a widely consumed Western-style dish in Singapore's cafés, bistros, and restaurants, as well as in home cooking. The dish consists of a fresh Atlantic salmon (Salmo salar) fillet — typically 180g — seasoned with lemon and herbs (parsley, dill, thyme) or butter, then baked in an oven until just cooked through. The flesh is tender, moist, and flaky with a mild, rich flavour and characteristic orange-pink colour from natural astaxanthin. Atlantic salmon sold in Singapore is predominantly farmed in Norway, Scotland, Tasmania (Australia), and increasingly from Singapore-based aquaculture facilities. Nutritionally, baked Atlantic salmon is one of the most clinically valuable single dishes available in Singapore's food environment. One 180g serving delivers: 38g complete protein (all essential amino acids), 3.4g omega-3 EPA+DHA (equivalent to 3–4 fish oil capsules and exceeding the AHA daily recommendation), 15mcg vitamin D (meeting the full daily adequate intake — critical given Singapore's high vitamin D deficiency prevalence in an indoor-working, sun-avoiding population), 882mg potassium (25% of the 3,500mg daily target), and virtually zero carbohydrate (glycaemic load = 0). The lemon-herb preparation saves approximately 130 kcal and 8g saturated fat per serving compared to butter preparation. Baked salmon is clinically recommended for Type 2 Diabetes (zero GL, omega-3 insulin sensitisation, vitamin D), hypertension (very low sodium 106mg per serving, high potassium, omega-3 blood pressure lowering), cardiovascular disease (omega-3 triglyceride reduction, cardioprotective), and rheumatoid arthritis (omega-3 anti-inflammatory, vitamin D, selenium). Fresh farmed Atlantic salmon is halal and gluten-free, and is available at halal-certified cafés throughout Singapore. Common search terms: baked salmon calories Singapore, salmon omega-3 content, 烤三文鱼 nutrition, salmon vitamin D, salmon fillet protein, salmon diabetes healthy.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods (Atlantic salmon, farmed, cooked dry heat) cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database. Both are approved primary sources. The food is a standard, well-characterised ingredient with extensive published nutritional data. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are supported by named approved sources.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as Atlantic salmon contains no digestible carbohydrate; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods. Sugar_g is naturally zero by the same basis. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a pure animal protein food product. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g carbohydrate per 100g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to 0 as a numeric sentinel value indicating not_applicable status for this negligible-carbohydrate food — GI testing is not performed on foods with no carbohydrate content. Monounsaturated_fat_g (4.4g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (3.9g) values are from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods for Atlantic salmon farmed cooked dry heat. Water_g (63.8g) is from USDA FoodData Central. Butter preparation variant adds approximately 130 kcal and 8g saturated fat per 180g serving; base nutrient values reflect lemon-herb preparation. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest defensible parent (Atlantic salmon, cooked); no exact FoodEx2 code exists for baked Atlantic salmon fillet with lemon-herb preparation. SNOMED CT code not identified for this food product. Dairy_free flag set to false as butter preparation variant contains dairy; lemon-herb preparation is dairy-free. Halal status applies to plain baked salmon without non-halal additives.
